mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of t-1mo suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of t-1mo provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales combined with falling housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 715417M and HOUST at 1256K as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 60% of co-occurrence instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index signals expanding productive capacity and improving supply-side conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as profit margins expand.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.5948 as of t-2mo shows elevated production levels
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO increases are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment combined with rising inflation signals stagflationary pressure that erodes real wages and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100K in 1 month AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159724K and CPIAUCSL at 321.5 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Joint decline in PAYEMS and rise in CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 55% of co-occurrence instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23512.717B as of t-6mo provides baseline for growth assessment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rate combined with rising inflation expectations signals monetary policy tightness that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.2% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 321.5 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 60% of co-occurrence instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment signals supply-side deterioration and labor market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.5948 and UNRATE at 4.1% as of t-2mo and t-1mo respectively
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of co-occurrence instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
